content: Eating disorders include Anorexia Nervosa (restrictive energy intake leading to significantly low weight, intense fear of gaining weight, disturbed body image), Bulimia Nervosa (recurrent binge eating with loss of control followed by compensatory behaviors like vomiting, laxatives, fasting, excessive exercise), and Binge Eating Disorder (recurrent binge eating without compensatory behaviors). Anorexia has highest mortality rate of any psychiatric disorder (~5-6% per decade) due to medical complications and suicide. Lifetime prevalence: anorexia ~0.6%, bulimia ~1-2%, BED ~2-3%. Pathophysiology involves genetic factors, neurobiological alterations in reward/restriction circuits, perfectionism traits, sociocultural pressures.
